description: Deferred review items tracked across all phases. Each entry maps to a source review comment that was deemed reasonable but deferred to a later phase. Check this file at each phase integration gate to identify items that may have been resolved as a side effect of other work.

items.item: Unify db.ts + store-driver.ts SQLite connections into single factory
items.detail: createStoreDriver() and createDb() each open their own bun:sqlite connection. If transaction() callback tries to write through a Drizzle client from createDb(), it will hit SQLITE_BUSY because BEGIN IMMEDIATE holds a write lock. Fix: consolidate into a single connection factory or add connection pooling.
items.notes: Task 3.17 disposition: deferred: requires DB connection refactor (Phase 4 store unification)

items.item: Add proper transaction isolation test using shared connection handle
items.detail: Test bypasses driver.transaction() - performs raw SQL insert outside the transaction callback. After connection unification (DEF-001), add a test that inserts via the driver-managed connection within a transaction callback and verifies commit/rollback semantics.
items.notes: Task 3.17 disposition: deferred: depends on DEF-001 connection unification

items.item: Populate killedByGrace field in ExitInfo when process is killed via grace-period SIGTERM→SIGKILL chain
items.detail: ExitInfo interface defines killedByGrace?: boolean but the field is never set. The doKill() flow sends SIGTERM first then SIGKILL after graceMs, but Exited promise always returns killedByGrace: undefined. Fix: track whether SIGKILL was sent after grace period expiry and expose it in ExitInfo. This becomes important in Phase 3 when downstream error classification (L1-L4) needs to distinguish between direct kills and grace-period timeouts for proper diagnostic messages.
items.notes: Task 3.17 disposition: deferred: subprocess metadata enhancement (Phase 3 abort chain)

items.item: Preserve doKill trigger source in ExitInfo for diagnostic and logging purposes
items.detail: doKill(trigger: string, forceSignal?: SignalName) receives trigger values ('timeout', 'abort_signal', 'manual_kill') but never stores or propagates them to ExitInfo. When Phase 3 implements real backend subprocess orchestration, the trigger source is needed for proper L1-L4 error classification and user-facing abort reason messages (e.g. 'killed by timeout' vs 'killed by user abort'). Fix: add trigger field to ExitInfo and populate it from doKill calls.
items.notes: Task 3.17 disposition: deferred: subprocess metadata enhancement (Phase 3 abort chain)

items.item: Add integration tests for graph() function with real pbxproj fixtures
items.detail: graph.test.ts currently only tests ProjectGraphSchema validation, not the graph() function itself. Add integration tests that invoke graph() with a ProjectDiscovery pointing to real fixture .xcodeproj directories, covering resolveXcodeprojPath for both direct project and workspace layouts. Deferred to Phase 2.2 to avoid duplicating fixture refactoring work.

items.item: Add fixture covering indirect dependency form (targetProxy → PBXContainerItemProxy → remoteGlobalIDString)
items.detail: Current project.pbxproj fixture only uses direct target=UUID references in PBXTargetDependency. Real Xcode projects commonly use the indirect form via targetProxy → PBXContainerItemProxy → remoteGlobalIDString. The parser code already handles this path but has no fixture/test coverage for it. Deferred to Phase 2.2 alongside DEF-005 (graph integration tests).

items.item: Add test coverage for indirect dependency resolution path in parsePbxproj
items.detail: The targetProxy → PBXContainerItemProxy → remoteGlobalIDString resolution chain in parsePbxproj has no dedicated unit test. Deferred to Phase 2.2 alongside DEF-005 and DEF-006 (file prioritisation to avoid fixture restructuring churn).

items.item: SolidJS reactivity: CandidateReviewPanel reads candidates/idx outside tracking scope
items.detail: In opentui-renderer.tsx CandidateReviewPanel, const candidates = s().candidates and const idx = s().candidateIndex are read once in the component body (outside JSX tracking scope). Subsequent state changes from toggle/navigate/reorder events won't reflect in the UI — the For each list, selection highlight, confirmed count, and edit preview all use stale values. Fix: either access s().candidates / s().candidateIndex directly in JSX expressions (reactive through Solid's auto-tracking), or wrap them in createMemo(() => s().candidates). Also consider adding eslint-plugin-solid to the project to catch future reactivity violations statically. Deferred to Phase 3 when the TUI renderer is refactored for real backend-driven state updates.
items.notes: Task 3.17 disposition: deferred: OpenTUI SolidJS reactivity (covered by DEF-025)

items.item: Intent.features uses required empty-array instead of optional per data flow doc
items.detail: Data Flow Specification §4 defines Intent fields as { goal, targetHint, targetKind?, deviceHint, features?, metricsHint?, scope }. Current IntentSchema declares features as z.array(z.string()) (required, defaults to []) rather than z.array(z.string()).optional(). Rationale: an empty array semantically means 'no features matched' which is equivalent to 'not specified' and simplifies parser logic. Revisit in Phase 3 when the Intent schema is integrated with LLM-based parsing (task 3.8) — the optional form may be more appropriate for LLM tool-calling marshalling.
items.notes: Task 3.17 disposition: deferred: schema alignment with LLM integration (Phase 3 runtime)

items.item: Intent uses metricsRequested (boolean) instead of metricsHint? (string) per data flow doc
items.detail: Data Flow Specification §4 defines metricsHint? as an optional string field. Current implementation uses metricsRequested: z.boolean(). Rationale: boolean is simpler for the rule-based parser — it only needs to know yes/no whether metrics were requested. A hint string would be more appropriate for LLM-based parsing (task 3.8) where the LLM could produce richer signals like 'fps+hitches' vs 'memory only'. Revisit in Phase 3 to either rename to metricsHint or add both fields.
items.notes: Task 3.17 disposition: deferred: schema alignment with LLM integration (Phase 3 runtime)

items.item: BackendPreferences.allowCrossTargetFallback is a no-op — field is exported and documented but never wired into select()/filterByTargetKind()/autoPick()
items.detail: BackendPreferences.allowCrossTargetFallback is exported and documented, but select()/filterByTargetKind()/autoPick() never read it. Rule 6 still always returns blocked.target_unsupported, so setting allowCrossTargetFallback: true has no effect. Either wire the fallback branch or mark the field/docstring as not implemented yet. Cross-target fallback (physical↔simulator) is an edge case relevant when a targetKind has no registered backends at all — this needs healthcheckGate integration (Phase 3.3/3.5) before wiring makes sense.
items.notes: Task 3.17 disposition: deferred: cross-target fallback wiring (backend capability G5)

items.item: Replace blocking Bun.spawnSync with async Bun.spawn in AppiumDeviceBackend and WdaManager
items.detail: AppiumDeviceBackend listDevices (L237), healthcheck (L295), listCrashes (L596) and WdaManager install (L133) use Bun.spawnSync in async methods, blocking the Bun event loop. This violates the architecture principle of 'different devices can be parallel' (§4). Fix: convert all 4 sites to Bun.spawn + await proc.exited, preserving existing stdout/stderr capture and exit-code handling. Requires G5 re-verify after change since devicectl calls are part of the physical device flow. Deferred because: (1) touches 4 functions across 2 files, (2) needs coordinated change to not break existing behavior, (3) G5 re-verification required.
items.resolved_by: post-review fix (Sisyphus session, Task 3.17) + G5 verified
items.notes: Task 3.17 disposition: deferred: requires G5 re-verify after async conversion (major)

items.item: Attach error context to fallback return values in AppiumDeviceBackend catch blocks
items.detail: 7 catch blocks in appium-device-backend.ts capture error messages into errorMsg but never use them: listApps (L349-352 returns []), getUiTree (L407-415 returns empty snapshot), screenshot (L437-446), startRecording (L553-560), stopRecording (L579-587), listCrashes (L635-638 returns []), collectLogs (L660-669). This violates R5 (no silent degradation). The return types (ArtifactRef, UiTreeSnapshot, RecordingHandle) lack an error field. Fix options: (a) console.error(errorMsg) for observability without changing contracts, or (b) extend return types with optional error field. Deferred because changing shared contract types (ArtifactRef, UiTreeSnapshot, RecordingHandle) affects all backend implementations and the engine layer.

items.item: Add AbortSignal propagation to WdaManager (WdaBuildOptions, WdaInstallOptions, WdaLaunchOptions)
items.detail: Architecture §4 requires abort to propagate through backend → child process via AbortSignal/Bun.spawn. WdaManager build/install/launch methods spawn subprocesses (xcodebuild, devicectl) but WdaBuildOptions/WdaInstallOptions/WdaLaunchOptions lack signal?: AbortSignal. Fix: add signal? to all 3 options interfaces, pass through to Bun.spawn calls. Deferred because: (1) needs coordination with SubprocessController (Task 1.15) for consistent signal handling, (2) requires AppiumDeviceBackend constructor to accept and forward AbortSignal to WdaManager, (3) best addressed when AgentRuntime (3.8) and ToolDispatcher (3.9) implement the full abort chain.
items.notes: Task 3.17 disposition: deferred: requires full AbortSignal chain implementation

items.item: Thread AbortSignal through DeviceBackend interface to enable in-flight cancellation
items.detail: ToolDispatcher checks AbortSignal before dispatch and before execution, but DeviceBackend methods (tap, swipe, screenshot, launchApp, etc.) lack a signal/options parameter. Running backend operations still cannot be cancelled mid-flight. Fix: add signal?: AbortSignal to the DeviceBackend contract methods, then pass this.signal into method.call() in ToolDispatcher, and propagate through every backend implementation (AppiumDeviceBackend, MockDeviceBackend, and future backends). Deferred because: (1) requires contract changes affecting all backend implementations, (2) needs coordination with DEF-012 (async Bun.spawn conversion) and DEF-014 (WdaManager AbortSignal propagation) for the complete signal chain from ToolDispatcher → backend → subprocess.
items.notes: Task 3.17 disposition: deferred: requires DeviceBackend contract change (major)

items.item: Replace console.error with redacting structured logger in AppiumDeviceBackend catch blocks
items.detail: DEF-013 was partially resolved by adding console.error(errorMsg) to 7 AppiumDeviceBackend catch blocks, but the current implementation logs raw error.message/String(error) directly. This violates R6 (sensitive data such as tokens, OTPs, payment data, and identifiers could appear in Appium driver error messages). Fix: introduce a redacting structured logger that strips PII before emitting, or log only safe operation context and error-class metadata (error name, HTTP status, operation name) rather than the raw error message. Add tests proving tokens, OTPs, payment data, and identifiers are removed from emitted logs. Deferred because: (1) no shared redacting logger infrastructure exists yet, (2) needs cross-cutting infrastructure decision (Phase 4 evidence/report layer), (3) current console.error is a strict improvement over the previous silent degradation.

items.item: XML entity encoding mismatch: findAlertButton/extractAlertButtons fail on labels containing '/&
items.detail: permissionAlertUiTree's deny button is encoded as label="Don't Allow", but DISMISS_BUTTONS contains the literal "Don't Allow". Since the search regex is built from the literal target and matched against un-decoded XML, this branch never matches — meaning a permission dialog offering only "Don't Allow" (Allow absent) would be reported as handled: false despite a valid dismiss button existing. extractAlertButtons has the same gap, surfacing encoded text instead of decoded labels. Fix: XML-decode entity references in the alert text before matching labels, and decode in extractAlertButtons so callers receive decoded labels. No current test exercises the deny-only path.
items.notes: Task 3.17 disposition: deferred: minor encoding fix (can be addressed in maintenance)

items.item: Add negative-path test coverage for device-explorer error branches
items.detail: All 20 tests exercise happy-path/degradation scenarios; none exercise MockToolDispatcher returning status: 'error' for tap/swipe/input/screenshot. The failStep branches at device-explorer.ts Lines 214-216, 248-250, 278-283, 294-296 are entirely untested. Given RunStep failure records feed downstream reports, adding tests that configure the mock to fail and assert the resulting failStep output would increase confidence in this path.
items.notes: Task 3.17 disposition: deferred: test coverage enhancement (Phase 4 hardening)

items.item: AC4 TUI assertion confirmation panel: Agent-suggested assertions need user confirmation flow
items.detail: US-11.1 AC4 requires Agent-suggested assertions to display evidence and request user confirmation before being used as tier-3 assertions. The AssertionEvaluator already supports the suggest/confirm contract (agentSuggestions→needs_assertion with suggestions field, agentConfirmed→full evaluation), but the TUI confirmation panel is not implemented yet. Current InteractiveRecorder has recording review panel (RecordingReviewPanel) that can serve as the base pattern. Full implementation requires AgentRuntime→LLM closed-loop (task 3.8/3.9 done but not yet end-to-end integrated) to generate assertion suggestions from exploration observations. Deferred to task 3.17 (Phase 3 integration) where the full harness (AgentRuntime→ToolDispatcher→DeviceExplorer→AssertionEvaluator→TUI) will be wired together. The contract interface (AssertionEvaluateOutput.suggestions) is stable and tested.
items.notes: Task 3.17 disposition: deferred: TUI assertion confirmation panel (Phase 3 harness wiring)

items.item: Keychain persistence in CredentialManager bypasses PermissionEngine (R7)
items.detail: CredentialManager stores remembered credentials directly to keychainStore (line 114-116) without going through PermissionEngine. test-plan-compiler.ts lists 'store_credential' in safety.highRiskActions, and AGENTS.md R7 requires secondary confirmation for high-risk operations. Fix: add PermissionEngine dependency to CredentialManager constructor, call permissionEngine.check({ action: 'store_credential', resource: key }) before keychainStore.set(). Deferred to Phase 3 integration (3.17) where PermissionEngine will be wired into the full harness. The TUI-level 'remembered' toggle provides user intent but does not satisfy the R7 architectural gate.

items.item: generateSearchKeyword category lookup uses feature names that don't match SEARCH_KEYWORDS keys
items.detail: context.features (e.g. 'login', 'search', 'profile') are used as keys into SEARCH_KEYWORDS (keyed by 'product', 'user_name', 'location', 'default'), so the lookup almost always falls back to 'default'. The returned TestDataItem.category retains the raw (unmatched) feature name, weakening the US-10.1 AC2 'project-aware generation' guarantee. Fix: add a feature→searchCategory mapping (e.g. { login: 'user_name', search: 'product' }) and resolve category before calling recordGet. Deferred because: needs cross-cutting feature vocabulary alignment with ProjectProfile feature names (Phase 2 definitions).
items.notes: Task 3.17 disposition: deferred: feature vocabulary alignment (Phase 3 runtime)

items.item: CredentialResolveResultSchema should use discriminated union for entry requiredness by status
items.detail: CredentialResolveResultSchema keeps entry as z.optional() regardless of status, even though it's only meaningful for 'found'/'prompted' statuses. Switching to z.discriminatedUnion('status', [...]) would require entry for those variants at the type level, preventing downstream code from having to guess when entry is present. The file already uses discriminatedUnion for TestDataItemSchema. Deferred because: schema change affects all CredentialResolveResult consumers (credential-manager.ts, test files, TUI components) and should be batch-refactored in 3.17 alongside DEF-020.
items.notes: Task 3.17 disposition: deferred: schema discriminated union (Phase 3 runtime)

items.item: G5 spike report 3.7 contradiction: §3d (Appium Session Creation) marked BLOCKED but overall status claims 7/7 PASS
items.detail: g5-spike-report-3.7.md line 84 marks Appium session creation as BLOCKED (free account limitation, Appium does not pass -allowProvisioningUpdates), but line 7 and 157 declare overall status as 7/7 PASS. The 7th verification target (Appium session creation) was satisfied via WDA direct xcodebuild bypass, not through the Appium adapter. The report should clarify this distinction: Appium-mediated session is blocked, but the direct WDA path works. Fix: update report to reflect tiered results (WDA-direct: PASS, Appium-mediated: BLOCKED) with clear rationale.
items.notes: Task 3.17: Documented in phase3-exit-report.md §5 Known Gaps. Report itself left unchanged as historical record.

items.item: US-7.1 (XCUITest path) ownership conflict between task 3.11 (BuildDriver) and task 4.1 (xcresult parser)
items.detail: US-7.1 xcodebuild test + xcresult is implemented in task 3.11 (build driver level), but xcresult parsing is assigned to task 4.1 (Phase 4 evidence/report). The split means Phase 3 cannot fully verify the XCUITest path end-to-end because result parsing is deferred. Resolution: either extend 3.11 to include basic xcresult parsing (test counts, pass/fail), or explicitly mark the XCUITest→xcresult→report chain as a Phase 3→Phase 4 handoff in task 3.17 exit criteria.
items.notes: Task 3.17: Handoff documented in phase3-exit-report.md — Phase 3 delivers xcodebuild test execution, Phase 4 delivers xcresult parsing. Cross-phase integration test verifies XCUITest path executes successfully.

items.item: OpenTUI renderer issues reported: nested <text> crash on native renderer, input focus not set, onSubmit not bound to keyboard events
items.detail: The report identifies specific OpenTUI renderer integration issues (packages/itestagent-tui/src/renderers/opentui-renderer.tsx). These are likely OpenTUI framework maturity issues rather than iTestAgent defects. The renderer tests also reportedly mock the real component tree, masking these issues. If OpenTUI cannot resolve these, the Ink fallback (ADR-008) should be activated. Resolution path: (a) investigate and fix if in our code, (b) report upstream if OpenTUI bug, (c) switch to Ink fallback if unresolvable.
items.notes: Task 3.17 disposition: deferred: OpenTUI third-party dependency (monitor upstream fixes)

items.item: Agent Harness internal inconsistencies: runtime/session/tool ID disunity, AI SDK wrapper empty tool call ID, tool.progress has no producer, BackendSelector healthcheck placeholder
items.detail: The report identifies several minor internal inconsistencies in the Agent Harness components (AiSdkAgentRuntime, ToolDispatcher, BackendSelector). These are not blocking defects but reduce internal coherence. Items: (1) ID generation not unified across runtime/session/tool layers, (2) AiSdkAgentRuntime may pass empty tool call IDs to AI SDK, (3) tool.progress AgentEvent type exists but no code produces it, (4) BackendSelector healthcheckGate may be a placeholder without real healthcheck integration. These should be systematically reviewed during task 3.17 integration testing when the full harness (Runtime→Dispatcher→Selector→Backend) is wired together.
items.notes: Task 3.17 disposition: Partially resolved — (2) AI SDK empty toolCallId fixed (crypto.randomUUID() fallback, commit 78cf280); (4) DeviceExplorer backend hardcode fixed (ExplorationOptions.backendName, commit 78cf280). Remaining items (1) ID unification, (3) tool.progress producer — deferred to Phase 4 polish.

items.item: US-11.1 assertion strategy conflict: P0 in task 3.14 AC1-AC3 but MVP summary lists P1 for assertion-based pass/fail
items.detail: The report notes a priority conflict: US-11.1 assertion strategy is listed as P0 in the task definition (3.14), suggesting assertions are critical for execution correctness, but the MVP completion standard (item 11) lists assertion-based pass/fail as P1 (explored/inconclusive/needs_assertion are acceptable outputs). Resolution: clarify in task 3.17 exit criteria that while the AssertionEvaluator exists (3.14), the full agent-suggested→confirmed→evaluated closed loop (including TUI confirmation panel per DEF-019) is Phase 3 integration scope, not individual task scope.
items.notes: Task 3.17: Clarified in phase3-exit-report.md — AssertionEvaluator exists (3.14), full closed-loop assert (LLM→confirm→evaluate) is Phase 3 harness wiring scope. explored/inconclusive/needs_assertion are valid Phase 3 outputs.
